home.hero_desc: An ancient village in the heart of the Gorno-Badakhshan Autonomous Region of Tajikistan, surrounded by majestic mountains and clear rivers.

sections.about_desc: Shitam is a small mountain village located in GBAO, Tajikistan. Friendly people preserve the ancient traditions of the Shughni people.
sections.nature_desc: Surrounded by mountain ranges and clear rivers, Shitam offers amazing Pamir landscapes.
sections.culture_desc: The Shughni language, traditional crafts and hospitality are the foundations of life in Shitam.

about.title: About Shitam Village
about.subtitle: History and Description
about.history_text: Shitam is one of the picturesque villages of the Gorno-Badakhshan Autonomous Region, located in the Pamir valley. The villagers have preserved the traditions, language and culture of the Shughni people for many centuries.
about.location_text: The village is located in GBAO, Tajikistan. Surrounded by mountain ranges, clear rivers and pristine Pamir nature.

nature.title: Nature of Shitam
nature.subtitle: Untouched beauty of the Pamirs
nature.mountains_desc: Shitam is surrounded by the majestic mountains of the Pamirs, whose peaks are covered with eternal snow.
nature.rivers_desc: Mountain rivers with transparent water create a unique landscape around the village.
nature.sky_desc: At Pamir altitude, the sky reveals thousands of stars — a sight you can never forget.

gallery.title: Gallery
gallery.subtitle: The beauty of Shitam in photographs

people.title: People & Culture
people.subtitle: The Soul of Shitam
people.shughni_desc: The residents of Shitam are Shughnis, one of the Pamir peoples with their own unique language and culture.
people.traditions_desc: Hospitality, respect for elders, traditional crafts and music are an integral part of life in Shitam.
people.ismaili_desc: Most residents practice Ismailism — a branch of Islam whose spiritual leader is the Aga Khan.

directions.title: How to Get There
directions.subtitle: Route to Shitam
directions.from_dushanbe_desc: By car along the M41 highway (Pamir Highway) through Kalai-Khumb or Khorog — about 500-600 km.
directions.from_khorog_desc: Khorog is the administrative center of GBAO. From Khorog to Shitam you can get by taxi or local transport.
directions.note: Foreign nationals require a special permit to visit GBAO.

footer.desc: Informational website about Shitam village, GBAO, Tajikistan.
